headline: Rural Sarawak Schools Report 42% Teacher Vacancy Rate
context: Sarawak Teachers Union reported 1,850 teaching positions in rural and interior schools remained unfilled as of January 2026.

cards.big: 1,850 teaching positions in rural Sarawak schools remain vacant
cards.sub: Some schools in Kapit and Baram operate with 3 teachers for 6 classes

cards.big: Ministry allocated only RM12 million for rural teacher incentives in 2026
cards.sub: Monthly hardship allowance of RM500 has not increased since 2008

cards.big: 67% of rural Sarawak students scored below national average in SPM 2025
cards.sub: Urban-rural gap in pass rates widened to 23 points from 18 in 2020

cards.big: Only 28% of interior schools have reliable internet for digital learning
cards.sub: Education Ministry Jendela programme reached 340 of 1,200 targeted schools

cards.big: Teacher vacancies expose whether education equity is a promise or a policy
42% teacher vacancies in rural Sarawak schools show that posting incentives alone do not work.
